title: 10 best herbal essential oils for motion sickness
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for motion sickness are a natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms of nausea, dizziness, and disorientation caused by travel or motion. These potent plant extracts work by calming the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety, and improving digestion. Peppermint, lavender, and ginger essential oils are popular choices for their soothing properties and ability to ease queasiness. For example, inhaling peppermint oil can help calm the stomach, while applying lavender oil to the wrists can promote relaxation. By using these herbal essential oils, individuals with motion sickness can enjoy travel without discomfort and improve overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with motion sickness because they contain natural compounds that have anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. These properties help to soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea, which are common symptoms of motion sickness. Additionally, ginger has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut, which can further contribute to feelings of queasiness. By using herbal ginger essential oils, individuals can find relief from motion sickness and enjoy a smoother travel experience.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant Property: Herbal ginger essential oil helps to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea by increasing motility in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.

Anti-Inflammatory Property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in herbal ginger essential oil help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to symptoms of motion sickness such as stomach upset and dizziness.

Antioxidant Property: Herbal ginger essential oil contains antioxidant compounds that help to neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress, which can exacerbate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds that help to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Rhizomes: Contains bioactive compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties to reduce symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger rhizomes of zingiber officinale in early morning for optimal oil quality and potency.
Wash the harvested ginger roots under cold running water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Dry the cleaned ginger roots in a warm well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Steam-distill the dried ginger roots in a steam distillation apparatus using 10 grams of ginger per liter of distilled water for 2 hours.
Filter and bottle the resulting essential oil immediately after distillation, storing it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with motion sickness because of its ability to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. The menthol content in peppermint oil helps to relax the digestive system, relieving feelings of queasiness and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's cooling properties help to ease tension and stress caused by motion sickness, promoting a sense of calmness and well-being. This natural remedy has been shown to effectively alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, making it an excellent option for those who suffer from this common affliction.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint essential oil can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by stimulating digestive enzymes and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of indigestion and upset stomach.

Anti-Inflammatory: The menthol content in peppermint oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to motion sickness symptoms like dizziness and lightheadedness.

Antioxidant and Antispasmodic: Peppermint essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the body from free radical damage caused by prolonged exposure to motion, while its antispasmodic effects can relax muscle spasms in the digestive tract, relieving cramps and discomfort associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other active compounds, peppermint roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Barks: Containing high amounts of camphor and menthone, peppermint bark helps soothe digestive issues and calm stomach discomfort associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves at dawn when their oil content is highest for optimal quality.
Dry the peppermint leaves in a warm dark place or using a food dehydrator on low heat.
Steam distill 100g of dried peppermint leaves with 500ml of distilled water for 30 minutes to extract essential oil.
Filter the resulting mixture through a coffee filter and store the separated essential oil in dark glass bottles.
Mix 10 drops of mentha x piperita essential oil with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil or jojoba oil as a topical remedy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with motion sickness because its calming and soothing properties can ease feelings of nausea and dizziness. The oil's gentle, floral scent can also help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for motion sickness. When inhaled, the essential oil's potent compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to stabilize the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing the symptoms of motion sickness. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from the discomforts of travel or adventure-related motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Calming and Sedative: English Lavender essential oil has a calming and sedative effect, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress associated with motion sickness.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of English Lavender essential oil may help to alleviate the discomfort and inflammation that can occur during motion sickness.
Digestive Soothing: English Lavender essential oil has been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help to ease nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and help alleviate nausea associated with motion sickness.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a unique combination of volatile compounds that can help stabilize the digestive system and reduce symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at dawn or just before flowering stage.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or air drying.
Distill the dried flowers in a copper still using a traditional hydrodistillation method for 4 hours.
Filter and decant the resulting essential oil into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids within an hour.
Store the final essential oil at room temperature away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with motion sickness because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate symptoms such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach discomfort. When applied topically or inhaled, fennel oil stimulates digestive enzymes and relaxes the muscles in the stomach, reducing spasms and cramps caused by motion. Additionally, its anxiolytic properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and unease often associated with motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Fennel essential oil's digestive stimulant property helps to calm nausea and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by improving digestion and reducing stomach upset.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of fennel essential oil help to relax the muscles in the stomach, intestines, and other organs, which can reduce cramping, discomfort, and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Calming and Sedative: Fennel essential oil's calming and sedative properties have a soothing effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety, stress, and dizziness that often accompany motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain a high concentration of natural compounds like estragole and anethole, which help to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have been traditionally used in aromatherapy to calm the stomach and reduce symptoms of motion sickness due to their carminative properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven on low heat for 4-6 hours or air-drying for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried foeniculum vulgare leaves using 1 part herb to 2 parts water for 30-40 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.
Store the foeniculum vulgare essential oil in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months or use within 3 months for maximum potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with motion sickness because its calming properties can alleviate symptoms of dizziness and nausea. When inhaled, chamomile's soothing aroma signals the body to relax and reduces anxiety, which can exacerbate motion sickness. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties may help ease stomach discomfort and relieve pressure on the inner ear, where motion sickness often originates. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, chamomile essential oils provide a natural and effective remedy for motion sickness sufferers.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Chamomile essential oil helps to calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress related to motion sickness.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic properties of chamomile essential oil can help to relax muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with motion sickness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract, which can be exacerbated by motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile contain apigenin, an flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to calm digestive issues associated with motion sickness.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality chamomile flowers and dry them thoroughly for 2 weeks.
Combine 100 grams of dried chamomile flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to allow for infusion and maceration.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Transfer the infused oil to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool place for 30 days before using it as an essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with motion sickness because of its natural properties that soothe and calm the stomach. The citrusy aroma of lemongrass oil has been shown to reduce nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of motion sickness. When inhaled or applied topically, lemongrass oil can help alleviate discomfort and anxiety associated with travel by car, boat, plane, or train. Its calming effect on the digestive system also helps to ease queasiness, making it an effective natural remedy for motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Lemongrass essential oil has been shown to relax muscles and reduce spasms, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Digestive stimulant: The oil's ability to stimulate digestion can help ease stomach upset and reduce the discomfort associated with motion sickness.
Insecticidal: Lemongrass essential oil has natural insect-repelling properties that may help prevent bites and stings that can trigger allergic reactions, which can exacerbate motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots are commonly used in traditional medicine due to their high concentration of citral, a compound known for its anti-nausea properties, making them effective in soothing motion sickness symptoms.
Buds: Lemongrass buds contain a higher concentration of citronellal, a natural compound that helps alleviate feelings of dizziness and disorientation often associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from your garden or local market in the morning.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with cold water and dry them using clean towels.
Steam-distill 50 grams of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves in 500 milliliters of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper into dark glass bottles, discarding the plant material residue.
Store the extracted cymbopogon citratus essential oil in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight and use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with motion sickness because of its unique properties that help to calm the mind and body. The oil contains eucalyptol, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, reducing muscle tension and nausea associated with motion sickness. Additionally, the oil's calming scent can help to soothe an upset stomach and promote a sense of relaxation, alleviating feelings of dizziness and disorientation. This makes it an effective natural remedy for individuals prone to motion sickness when traveling by car, boat, or plane.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic properties: The oil contains compounds like cinnamic acid and borneol that have been shown to reduce nausea and vomiting in individuals experiencing motion sickness.
Anxiolytic properties: The calming effects of the oil's constituents, such as α-pinene and limonene, can help alleviate anxiety and stress related to motion sickness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce discomfort and pain associated with motion sickness, which is often accompanied by headaches, fatigue, and dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian blue gum contain compounds that help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Barks: The barks of the tree are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning and wash them gently with cold water.
Steam distill the eucalyptus globulus leaves using a still with 10 liters of distilled water for 4 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the steam distillation process in a glass container and store it at room temperature.
Add a few drops of the eucalyptus globulus essential oil to a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 5% essential oil to 95% carrier oil.
Mix well and apply topically to the wrists, temples, or behind the ears as needed for motion sickness relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with motion sickness because it contains a compound called limonene, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled, limonene can help to reduce nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness by calming the digestive system and regulating stomach acids. Additionally, bitter orange essential oil's citrusy aroma can also stimulate the brain's limbic system, helping to reduce symptoms of motion sickness and promote a sense of calmness and relaxation. This natural remedy can provide relief from motion sickness without relying on pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Bitter orange oil helps the body adapt to stress and anxiety, which is often a contributor to motion sickness, by promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of overwhelm.

Digestive benefits: Bitter orange oil has been shown to soothe digestive issues, such as nausea and stomach discomfort, which are common symptoms of motion sickness, by improving digestion and reducing inflammation in the gut.

Anxiolytic properties: Bitter orange oil contains compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and panic associated with motion sickness, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bitter orange plant have been traditionally used in herbal remedies for digestive issues, including nausea and vomiting, making them suitable for treating motion sickness.
Buds: The buds of the bitter orange plant contain high levels of flavonoids, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and disorientation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried citrus peel from sweet orange or bitter seville oranges in early spring.
Grind the dried citrus peel into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 10 seconds.
Combine the powdered citrus peel with 200ml of high-proof vodka and 100g of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for infusion and decarboxylation processes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container and discard the solids then bottle and label.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon essential oils helps with motion sickness because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as dizziness, nausea, and vomiting. The citrus scent of lemon oil also has a calming effect on the mind and body, which can reduce feelings of anxiety and stress caused by motion sickness. Additionally, lemon oil's digestive benefits can help soothe an upset stomach and relieve discomfort associated with travel-induced nausea.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Herbal lemon essential oil's digestive stimulant property helps to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by stimulating digestion and relieving stomach upset.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory: The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of herbal lemon essential oil may help reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms, such as headaches and dizziness.

Anxiolytic (Anxiety-Relieving): The anxiolytic property of herbal lemon essential oil can help calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress levels associated with motion sickness, which can contribute to nausea and other uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon plant contain compounds that help to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea, making them effective in treating motion sickness.
Rhyzomes: Lemon rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the stomach and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Buds: The buds of the lemon plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which work together to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, making them effective in treating motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Steam distill freshly harvested dried lemons for one hour.
Combine equal parts of lemon balm and ginger roots in a mixing bowl.
Add 20 drops of eucalyptus oil to the blended herbs for enhanced properties.
Allow the blend to steep for 30 minutes in hot water before filtering.
Transfer the clear liquid into a glass bottle for storage and later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tarragon essential oils helps with motion sickness because of its unique composition that contains a calming and soothing effect on the digestive system. The oil's anise-like aroma has been shown to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness, making it a natural remedy for those who frequently experience seasickness or car sickness. By reducing symptoms of discomfort and anxiety, tarragon essential oils can help provide relief from motion-related distress, allowing individuals to travel comfortably without worry.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Tarragon essential oil helps to ease digestive discomfort, nausea, and bloating associated with motion sickness by reducing gas and flatulence in the stomach.
Anxiolytic property: The oil's calming effect helps to alleviate stress, anxiety, and feelings of unease caused by motion sickness, promoting relaxation and reducing emotional discomfort.

Antiemetic property: Tarragon essential oil has been shown to possess anti-nausea properties, which can help to reduce the frequency and severity of vomiting episodes commonly experienced during motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tarragon roots are used for their calming and grounding properties, making them effective in reducing nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.
Leaves: The leaves of the tarragon plant contain a high concentration of volatile oils that help to soothe and calm the digestive system, alleviating symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia dracunculus leaves in the early morning dew to ensure maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloth for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried artemisia dracunculus leaves using a copper still and high-quality essential oil carrier medium at a ratio of 1:4.
Collect the distillate after 2 hours of steaming, filter it through cheesecloth to remove impurities, and store it in dark glass bottles.
Allow the filtered essential oil to mature for 24-48 hours before using or bottling for use as a motion sickness remedy.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat motion sickness with herbal essential oils is by using a blend of calming and digestive herbs. Lavender oil helps reduce anxiety and nausea, while ginger oil has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach. Peppermint oil also relieves nausea and can help ease stomach discomfort. Apply 5-7 drops of the blend to your wrists or temples, inhale deeply, and feel relief from motion sickness symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to motion sickness that are treated with herbal essential oils are nausea, vertigo, and labyrinthitis. Peppermint oil is often used to alleviate nausea and digestive issues, while lavender oil can help soothe vertigo and promote relaxation. Ginger oil has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce symptoms of labyrinthitis, a condition characterized by inner ear problems and dizziness. These essential oils can be inhaled or applied topically to provide relief from these conditions.
